changes.reason: The current call-wall profile is convex but tail-heavy: 2 trades, $640.5 PnL, best standalone efficiency, but global worst MAE -655.2 and 66-minute median hold. Reducing the adaptive cap from 6 to grid value 5 tests whether this profile can monetize early favorable movement before the long underwater recovery path develops.

The worst call-wall trade had early positive progress and first +3 points within minutes, but used a very wide effective target near 20.6 points and reached TP only after 120 minutes. A 4.5-point fixed profile target is the least aggressive batch-A cap in the allowed grid that can convert early continuation into a shorter hold without disabling the edge.

Raise strike acceptance from 20 to 45 seconds to require more durable call-wall acceptance before this option-confirmed profile enters, while avoiding a blunt DEX or volume tightening that could delete the tiny sample outright.

expected_effect: Should reduce call_wall_acceptance_long hold time, worst MAE, and drawdown contribution by taking earlier profits on accepted call-wall moves. Main tradeoff is lower raw PnL from the rare convex call-wall winner; reject if total net profit falls below 98% of run_044 or if call_wall_acceptance_long loses most of its standalone contribution without materially improving worst MAE/drawdown.
label: promote
rationale: Run_044 is the score and net-profit leader, but its incremental edge came with worse worst MAE and drawdown. The culprit is not the core pullback or charm-pin book; it is the two-trade call_wall_acceptance_long sleeve, especially the $546 winner that also carried -655.2 MAE and a 120-minute path to TP. Batch A is the correct single-family test: preserve the option-confirmed call-wall concept, avoid global veto tightening, and test profile-local realization plus slightly firmer strike acceptance rather than deleting the profile.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: call_wall_acceptance_long produced only 2 VALID_0DTE trades but contributed $640.5, $290.3/hour, and the global worst MAE at -655.2. The worst trade had early favorable excursion but a very wide target and long recovery path.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Call-Wall Early Realization Containment
strategy_hypotheses.risk: The profile may require wide targets to pay for rare convex call-wall moves; capping TP can improve MAE and exposure while suppressing total net profit.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: VALID_0DTE call-wall acceptance with positive forward DEX and strike acceptance, but wide selected target distance and extended ES-VWAP state.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: call_wall_acceptance_long worst MAE improves materially, total worst MAE moves toward <= -513.4, slow >=12h stays 0, total net profit remains >=98% of run_044, and portfolio PnL/exposure stays flat or better.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: The analyzer repeatedly shows static config cannot distinguish delayed convex recoveries from clean fast winners as well as intratrade path features: first profit timing, half-TP touch, and MFE-before-MAE separate tail behavior.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Call-Wall Convex Recovery Monitor
strategy_hypotheses.risk: Path-aware logic can overfit a two-trade profile unless validated against protected fast winners and out-of-sample days.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: call_wall_acceptance_long enters with wide target and extended ES-VWAP but fails to make early half-target progress or prints adverse expansion before meaningful continuation.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Reduce call_wall_acceptance_long worst MAE while retaining at least 70% of its PnL, keeping total score flat or better, and preserving protected fast-winner retention >=95%.

strategy_plan_reference.market_regime_read: Short, high-win-rate, take-profit-only sample over 12.3 calendar days. Realized loss rate is 0% and slow >=12h trades are 0, so the risk-reducer problem is hidden MAE and capita...

new_strategy_ideas.name: Early Progress Heat Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Worst MAE improves toward <= -513.4, average MAE improves, protected fast-winner retention >=95%, total net profit >=98% of run_044, slow >=12h remains 0.

new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Split
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= $1315.3, 1DTE average MAE improves from -124.6, worst MAE no worse than -386.75, total score flat or better.

new_strategy_ideas.name: Dormant Charm-Wall Pin-Avoid Attribution Probe
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Activated dormant profile positive standalone PnL or lower combined charm/pin worst MAE, total net profit >=98% of run_044, protected fast-winner retention >=95%.

new_strategy_ideas.name: Call-Wall Convex Recovery Monitor
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: call_wall_acceptance_long worst MAE improves materially while retaining >=70% of its PnL and total portfolio score does not degrade.
